notes: Bridge map for model theorem -> executable conformance op evidence. The registry remains narrower than universal mechanized refinement, but `retarget_v1` and `parse_tx` are now backed by machine-checked Go-trace contracts instead of metadata-only baseline evidence. parse_tx drift exception (PARSE-16) is payload-pinned via triple-segment content hash (~192-bit collision resistance). PV structural proofs added in ParallelEquivalence.lean (Q-PV-19).

critical_ops.contract_scope: All 16 CV-PARSE Go-trace vectors replayed against Lean parseTx. Exact id set pinned by parseExpectedIds (PARSE-01..PARSE-16). PARSE-16 drift exception payload-pinned via triple-segment content hash.
limitations: Narrow trace-backed contract over current 16 CV-PARSE fixture vectors, not a universal proof for all possible transaction byte sequences.

PARSE-16 has documented error-priority drift (Go: WITNESS_OVERFLOW, Lean: SIG_ALG_INVALID). Drift exception is pinned to concrete fixture payload via triple-segment hash (full + first-half + second-half, ~192-bit collision resistance) — any byte change breaks the proof.
Serialize direction (Lean→bytes→Go roundtrip) is not covered by this contract.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Generated Go trace v1 rows with `op=retarget_v1`, replayed against Lean `PowV1.retargetV1` on matching `CV-POW` fixture ids.
limitations: This is a narrow trace-backed equivalence contract, not a universal proof for all possible retarget inputs.
Other critical ops remain baseline-only until they receive their own machine-checked bridge.

critical_ops.contract_scope: All 10 CV-FORK-CHOICE vectors with op=fork_choice_select (6 positive + 4 negative) replayed against selectWinner which exercises chainTotalWork + bytesLT tie-break. Exact id set pinned by forkChoiceSelectExpectedIds. Proved by native_decide, axiom-free — no block_hash_collision_resistance in the theorem dependency chain.
limitations: Narrow trace-backed contract over current 10 CV-FORK-CHOICE fork_choice_select vectors, not a universal proof for all possible chain configurations.
Data source is CV-FORK-CHOICE conformance vectors, not Go trace (go_trace_v1.jsonl does not contain fork_choice_select operations).

forkSelect model theorems (determinism, antisymmetry) in ForkChoiceSelect.lean still use block_hash_collision_resistance axiom for universal proofs; this contract supplements them with concrete axiom-free evidence.
Equivalence with Go/Rust implementation is structural (same if/else chain), not proved via rfl.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal inductive proof: for ANY transaction list and ANY UTXO state, if `connectBlockTxs` succeeds then UTXO conservation AND anti-double-spend hold simultaneously. Chain-level composition via `utxo_apply_basic_chain_refinement`. No axioms, no fixture dependence.
limitations: `CV-U-16` is intentionally excluded from the narrow contract because it is a post-activation SLH row while the current formal model remains pre-rotation.
The universal theorems cover `connectBlockTxs` (non-coinbase transaction processing). Header linkage, PoW, and coinbase checks are proved elsewhere.
The bridge fails closed on id-set drift in the narrow contract layer.

critical_ops.notes: validateWitnessErrors extracted from parseDATx as LIVE sub-function. Exhaustive 4-code error taxonomy proved (validateWitnessErrors_taxonomy). All parseDATx error codes now machine-checked: TX_ERR_PARSE from structure, TX_ERR_WITNESS_OVERFLOW/TX_ERR_SIG_ALG_INVALID/TX_ERR_SIG_NONCANONICAL from validateWitnessErrors.
